showcase_features.title: Know what is happening on your phone.
showcase_features.description: Turn device health into an explainable report instead of digging through settings screens.
check_items: Memory, storage, battery, and network status
App permission and sensitive-permission review
Active app and hidden-app signal checks

showcase_features.title: Turn phone noise into a daily brief.
showcase_features.description: FoneClaw summarizes SMS, notifications, and system information so important items are not buried across apps.
check_items: Summarize SMS by contact and time
Search information by topic
View context and mark items as handled

showcase_features.title: Change Android settings faster.
showcase_features.description: Use natural language for supported Android settings instead of manually navigating deep menus.
check_items: Brightness, auto brightness, screen timeout, rotation
Media, ringtone, notification, alarm, call, and system volume
Silent mode, Do Not Disturb, flashlight, and system setting pages

showcase_features.title: Move from intent to a phone result.
showcase_features.description: FoneClaw handles configured email, calendar, alarms, local notes, location, nearby search, map navigation, and web tasks.
check_items: Email requires IMAP/SMTP setup
Maps require an installed map app
Web comparison uses web-derived information, not guaranteed real-time prices

showcase_features.title: Control Wi-Fi and Bluetooth by voice.
showcase_features.description: Manage wireless connections without digging through Android settings. Scan, connect, pair, and forget from a single command.
check_items: Wi-Fi: scan nearby, connect, forget, saved networks
Bluetooth: pair, connect, view paired and connected devices
Works with headphones, speakers, cars, and wearables

showcase_features.title: FoneClaw works even when you do not open the app.
showcase_features.description: When you take a screenshot or photo in supported Android workflows, FoneClaw can detect available context and offer a summary in a floating window. No context switch needed.
check_items: Screenshot auto-detection and summary
Photo auto-detection and content analysis
Floating window overlay, stays out of your way
Requires detection and overlay permissions

trust_items.title: Transparent permissions
check_items: Accessibility for screen reading and node actions
Notification access for notification summaries
Contacts, SMS, location, camera, Bluetooth, overlay, and system-setting permissions where needed

trust_items.title: Confirmation for sensitive actions
trust_items.description: Dialing, sending SMS, sending email, deleting records, and other sensitive actions should require confirmation before execution.

trust_items.title: Setup requirements are clear
trust_items.description: Email needs IMAP/SMTP configuration. Navigation needs an installed map app. Screenshot/photo auto-summary needs detection and overlay permissions. Some Android settings need extra authorization.

features_faq.question: What can FoneClaw control on Android?
features_faq.answer: FoneClaw supports 120+ current Android phone actions across 16 categories: device checks, notification and SMS summaries, calls, system settings, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, screenshots and photos, screen reading, email, calendar, alarms, local notes, maps, and web tasks.

features_faq.question: Does FoneClaw need Accessibility permission?
features_faq.answer: Yes. Accessibility is required for screen reading, node-based clicks, notification panel operations, and some phone-level interactions that cannot be handled by standard app permissions alone.

features_faq.question: Can FoneClaw send SMS or email automatically?
features_faq.answer: FoneClaw can draft, prepare, and execute supported communication actions, but sensitive operations such as sending messages or email should require user confirmation before final execution.

features_faq.question: Does every feature work without setup?
features_faq.answer: No. Email needs IMAP/SMTP setup, maps need an installed map app, image auto-summary needs the relevant detection and overlay permissions, and some Android settings require additional system permissions. Core features like phone status, SMS summary, and system settings work out of the box.

features_faq.question: What features are available?
features_faq.answer: FoneClaw covers 16 feature categories with 120+ supported actions: device checks, notification and SMS summaries, calls, system settings, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, screenshots and photos, screen reading, email, calendar, alarms, local notes, maps, web tasks, workflows, and quick commands.
